lesson: A sled of mass m is released from rest at the top of a snowy hill that makes an angle θ with the horizontal. The sled slides down the incline, as shown. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the sled and the hill is μ_k. Take g = 9.8 m/s^2. Let x be the distance measured along the incline from the release point. (a) Find Fnet, the magnitude of the net force acting on the sled along the incline. (b) Find v_x, the speed of the sled when it reaches position x on the incline.
